figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of the SAGR framework. The semantic reasoning layer assigns robots to rooms using an LLM operating on the semantic area graph. The frontier selection layer allocates frontier clusters and optimizes waypoint order, while the local execution layer performs navigation and sensing.

Fig. 2. Example semantic area graph constructed from the observed semantic map. Nodes represent discovered room instances and edges represent spatial adjacency. Each node stores room attributes including frontier clusters, robots, and neighboring rooms.
Fig. 3. Example environment from the HM3D dataset, showing an indoor apartment scene with semantic room layouts used for our search experiments.
